text.title: field-descriptions-as-prompts Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Field with no description at all.
antipattern.reason: Model interpolates from priors; format compliance drops 10-30 pp on the field.

section.p: Symptom: high mis-fill rate on the field. Root cause: missing description. Fix: add 1-3 sentences with format and one edge case.

antipattern.description: Description is the field name written out (`age` → "age", `total` → "total").
antipattern.reason: Adds tokens without information; the description fails its job as a micro-prompt.

section.p: Symptom: schema review marks descriptions as "fine" but compliance does not improve. Root cause: no actual content. Fix: replace with format / units / edge case.

antipattern.description: Description tells the model to "explain why" or "show your work".
antipattern.reason: Reasoning then leaks into the value field, corrupting the answer shape; the field becomes prose, not a value.

section.p: Symptom: value field contains prose instead of the expected type. Root cause: reasoning ask in description. Fix: split into reasoning + answer fields per `embedded-scratchpad-field`.

antipattern.description: "The X for this object" template descriptions.
antipattern.reason: Vague phrasing leaves the model guessing units and edges; lift collapses.

section.p: Symptom: descriptions look professional but compliance is flat. Root cause: template instead of content. Fix: concretise — name the type of X and its units explicitly.

antipattern.description: Description longer than ~3 sentences or written as flowing paragraphs.
antipattern.reason: Wastes the prompt-cache budget; model parses bullet-style hints just as well in 1/3 the tokens.

section.p: Symptom: schema cold-cache cost is high; description tokens dominate. Root cause: prose. Fix: tighten to 1-3 sentences; use bullet-shaped phrases ("X. Y. Z.").

antipattern.description: Description contradicts the field name or type (`amount: float` described as "string of cents").
antipattern.reason: Model picks one of the two interpretations randomly; consumers receive mixed shapes.

section.p: Symptom: mixed-type rows in production data. Root cause: conflict between two sources of truth. Fix: rename the field; do not paper over with a description.
